expectations.evidence: Line 63: alert = demisto.alert(). No demisto.incident() present — model correctly knows to use demisto.alert() for XSIAM without the skill.

After alert = demisto.alert() on line 63, the code immediately calls alert.get('src_ip', '').strip() (line 67) with NO CustomFields flattening. The pop/update pattern is absent. If src_ip or dest_ip live in CustomFields (which they often do in XSIAM), this script will silently return empty strings.

Line 18: return demisto.executeCommand('ip', {'ip': ip}) — uses the raw low-level API instead of the execute_command() CommonServerPython alias. This requires manual isError() checking (done on line 30) rather than automatic exception raising.
